verdict_reasoning: STIM1 (Stromal Interaction Molecule 1) is a single-pass type I transmembrane protein resident in the ER membrane. Critically, it also has a documented stable pool at the plasma membrane, where its short extracellular N-terminal domain (with an EF-hand-like SAM domain on the luminal/extracellular side) faces the extracellular space. The PM pool of STIM1 is detectable by surface biotinylation and antibody staining on intact cells even at rest, and is further enriched at ER-PM junctions during store-operated calcium entry (SOCE) signaling. Antibodies targeting the extracellular domain of STIM1 have been developed and used in live-cell assays. The extracellular EF-hand / cEF domain is genuinely exposed on the outer leaflet via the single-pass TM topology.
